message:
The other day, I was in the kitchen, trying to whip up a quick dinner, when I suddenly remembered the fire safety talk we had at the community center—good ol’ smoke alarms and escape plans. It got me thinking about how much we rely on gadgets and tech these days. 

So, I decided to have a little fun without screens. I gathered the kids and we crafted a fire escape plan using good old-fashioned paper and crayons. We even turned it into a game, pretending to be fire explorers on a mission to find the safest way out. 

Not only did we have a blast, but it also brought us closer together and reminded us of the importance of being prepared—without all the tech distractions. Plus, the kids now know exactly where to go if we ever need to escape. 

Rediscovering these low-tech moments can be such a joy, don’t you think? What fun ways have you found to teach safety to your family?
